objective: Let $k\geq 2$. Is it true that, for any distinct integers $1<n_1<\cdots <n_k$ such that\[1=\frac{1}{n_1}+\cdots+\frac{1}{n_k}\]we must have $\max(n_{i+1}-n_i)\geq 3$?
completion_criteria: Official database status is falsifiable; refine the exact certificate contract before any candidate claim.
non_results: Finite computation outside its stated range is not a proof of a universal claim.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.
Restating a known reduction, checking examples, or giving an unproved key lemma is not terminal success.

baseline_review.requirement: Repeat when the authoritative status changes materially or the baseline is explicitly invalidated.

synthesis_summary: The maintained positive-denominator statement remains open/falsifiable. The historical signed identity is outside its scope. The linked Lean file contains sorry in the main, gap-2, prime, and bridge theorems. The certificate-contract experiment checked 5,798 sequences with zero mismatches and passed seven controls in 0.288 seconds with 18,164 KB peak child memory. A pinned good-prime repository's arithmetic also reran successfully, but its lower bound remains conditional because the mathematical bridge was not independently proved. No frontier search was launched.

established_facts.claim: The current official target requires positive denominators beginning with 1 < n_1 and is labelled FALSIFIABLE.
established_facts.evidence: Official page, revision history, and teorth database commit a7b9502af8962b83161f403d55dddb1226e538d0

established_facts.claim: The historical signed 20-term vector sums to 1 and has gaps at most 2, but fails the present positivity hypothesis.
established_facts.scope: That displayed vector under the current statement

established_facts.claim: If exactly one denominator has strictly maximal p-adic valuation, the reciprocal sum cannot be an integer.
established_facts.evidence: After multiplication by the LCM, exactly one numerator summand is nonzero modulo p.
established_facts.scope: Any finite collection of positive integer denominators

established_facts.claim: The Fraction checker and LCM oracle agree on all subsequences of 2 through 14 with 2 through 7 terms.
The pinned good-prime code verifies its chain, Pocklington steps, and numerical floors.
established_facts.scope: Repository arithmetic only; assumes the mathematical bridge

strategies.title: Mandatory source/status baseline audit followed by exact validation of the finite-counterexample certificate contract
strategies.mechanism: Recognize finite disproof witnesses with exact Fraction arithmetic and audit that recognizer against a separate integer LCM-clearing implementation
strategies.hypothesis: The Fraction-based witness checker agrees with an independent LCM-clearing oracle on every subsequence of 2 through 14 having 2 through 7 terms and rejects controls for the corrected scope.
strategies.discriminating_test: Compare both exact encodings on all 5,798 bounded sequences and stop at the first mismatch or failed gap, positivity, sum, or ordering control.
strategies.blocker: The theorem-strength blocker is an independently verified good-prime bridge. Formal verification is additionally blocked because the prescribed Mathlib cache cannot unpack with only 2.8 GB free.
strategies.reopen_condition: For Lean, provide at least 7 GB free or a larger PROOF_FACTORY_CACHE_DIR and complete the bootstrap. For chain extension, first supply an independent proof or formal verification of the bridge.

strategies.mechanism: Retrieve existing Mathlib valuation and finite-sequence lemmas, prove the unique-maximal-valuation obstruction, and assemble the good-prime bridge before importing finite chain data.
strategies.hypothesis: The forum bridge can be stated and proved for arbitrary admissible p,q,M without any hypothesis beyond positivity, small gaps, endpoint inequalities, and the stated primality conditions.
strategies.discriminating_test: A standalone theorem compiles without sorry and its axiom report contains only accepted Mathlib foundations, with explicit tests of q=7, q=11, and interval endpoints.
strategies.rationale: This attacks the logical bottleneck directly and has far higher information value than another prime-chain extension.
strategies.title: Retrieve existing Mathlib valuation and finite-sequence lemmas, prove the unique-maximal-valuation obstruction, and assemble the good-prime bridge before importing finite chain data.

open_leads.rationale: It is the missing theorem behind the strongest existing finite certificate.
open_leads.next_experiment: Prove the unique-maximal-valuation lemma and p/q bridge in a standalone Lean module, requiring no sorry and an inspected axiom report.

open_leads.description: Attack all bridge boundary cases with an independent LCM proof and PARI/GP.
open_leads.rationale: This can cheaply expose a small-q or endpoint omission before formalization.
open_leads.next_experiment: Enumerate admissible small p, q, and M configurations and all subsets of q, 2q, and 3q; stop at the first failure of the predicted valuation obstruction.

open_leads.description: Audit the reported k <= 18 Lean development if it becomes available.
open_leads.rationale: Its fixed-k decision theorem and gap-word bridge may be reusable, but the forum description is not evidence.
open_leads.next_experiment: Build the exact archive, search for sorry/admit and unexpected axioms, and compare its theorem statement with the official source.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Use the signed-denominator identity to disprove the current problem.
ruled_out.evidence: Official history and exact scope control
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The official statement would have to revert to signed denominators.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Interpret formalized: yes as a proof.
ruled_out.reason: The main and bridge declarations contain sorry.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A compiling, sorry-free theorem with an acceptable axiom report.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Extend the good-prime chain during the next epoch.
ruled_out.reason: A longer chain only enlarges a conditional cutoff while the decisive bridge remains unverified.
ruled_out.evidence: Pinned arithmetic replay and source audit
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Independent written or formal proof of the bridge.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Repeat the forum's n_1 <= 12 or k <= 18 computations as a standalone contribution.
ruled_out.reason: Their artifacts are unavailable and repeating arbitrary cutoffs has no credible standalone acceptance path.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A supplied archive requiring independent review, or use as a regression control for a stronger structural result.

next_session.objective: Independently verify the arbitrary-parameter good-prime bridge before any chain extension.
next_session.first_action: Run `df -BG /`; if at least 7G is free, run `/root/proof-factory/scripts/bootstrap-formal-conjectures.sh`; otherwise begin the standalone LCM proof and PARI/GP boundary-control script without another bootstrap attempt.
next_session.stop_condition: Stop on any admissible p,q,M counterexample or missing hypothesis; otherwise stop when a sorry-free bridge compiles with an inspected axiom report, or when a complete paper proof and boundary controls isolate the exact remaining formal lemma.
